title: Deep-fried fish and chips
excerpt: Look at Sandra, she's got style. When she asked me to make a plain deep-fried fish, I knew I'd have to go all out and show off. It's hard to get really crispy fish. I discovered the secret! Read carefully!

seo.title: Deep-fried fish and chips - Marinda Kook

html: Look at Sandra, she's got style.  When she asked me to make a plain deep-fried fish, I knew I'd have to go all out and show off. It's hard to get really crispy fish. I discovered the secret! Read carefully!

ingredientGroups.title: Thin crispy chips
items: 4 large potatoes (choose potatoes suitable for chips)

directionGroups.title: Beer batter – Fish
steps: Take the fish (still frozen) out of the plastic and dip the pieces one by one quickly into a bowl of boiling water.
Mix 250 ml flour with salt and pepper in a bowl and press the pieces one by one into the mixture.
Put baking paper on a baking tray and let the fish rest on it for about 15 minutes.
In another bowl mix 250 ml flour, baking powder, corn flour, salt and white pepper.
Pour the oil into a pot and heat it up well.
Dip the pieces in the beer batter and lower them into the hot oil.
Once nicely browned, drain on kitchen paper.

directionGroups.title: Thin crispy chips
steps: Peel the potatoes and cut them into thin slices.
Put the raw slices into ice water.
Dry the slices and fry them for about 4 minutes at 150 °C.
Remove and drain on kitchen paper.
Heat the oil to 200 °C and fry the chips until golden brown.
Drain on kitchen paper and sprinkle with coarse salt.

directionGroups.title: Tartare sauce
steps: Mix everything and put it in the fridge for at least 30 minutes.
